Plan: add or correct “X11UseLocalhost yes” in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf. If the file is missing, create it with the line. Follow workflow: PLAN → review_plan → execute.

Plan: check for `/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf`. If missing, create it with permissions 0600. Ensure the line `X11UseLocalhost yes` is present exactly once: delete any existing `X11UseLocalhost` entries (commented or not) and append the correct line. Use `sed -i` for in‑place editing, `read_file` to verify current content, and `write_file` only when creating the file. This avoids duplicate lines and maintains proper file ownership.

If it does exist, I remove any existing lines that set X11UseLocalhost (commented or not) using `sed -i '/^\s*#\?\s*X11UseLocalhost\s\+/Id'`. After cleaning, I append the correct setting to the end of the file. All actions are performed as separate commands to avoid chaining.

I will first use `read_file` to inspect the target file. If the file exists, I will proceed to modify it with `sed` and `echo`; otherwise, I will create it with `write_file`. The plan is sequential: read, decide, then write or edit accordingly.
